title: diplacus glutinosus
intro: Diplacus glutinosus, also known as the sticky monkey flower, is a plant species native to the western United States and parts of Canada. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe and heal wounds, as well as treat respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. The medicinal properties of Diplacus glutinosus are attributed to the presence of glycosides, which have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial and antioxidant activities. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, or salves to harness its health benefits, including reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing.
